diagrams.title: Binary Heap Structure: Tree and Array Representation
diagrams.description: Shows a max-heap visualized as both a complete binary tree and its corresponding array representation. Each node contains a number, with the heap property that parent is greater than children. Array indices show parent-child relationships: parent at i has children at 2i+1 and 2i+2.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental data structure used in heapsort - how the binary heap is stored and the heap invariant that enables efficient sorting

diagrams.title: Heapsort Algorithm Steps
diagrams.description: Illustrates the three main phases of heapsort: (1) Build max-heap from unsorted array using heapify from bottom-up, (2) Repeatedly swap root (maximum) with last unsorted element, (3) Reduce heap size and restore heap property via heapify. Shows arrows indicating element swaps and heapify operations.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the step-by-step process of heapsort algorithm, showing how the max-heap property enables extracting maximum elements in sorted order

diagrams.title: Randomized Heapsort: Random Shuffling Effect
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the randomization aspect in heapsort variants. Shows an unsorted array being randomly permuted (Fisher-Yates shuffle) before heap construction. Compares worst-case vs average-case scenarios with different input orders. Highlights how randomization guards against pathological inputs.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how randomization is applied to heapsort to achieve expected O(n log n) time regardless of input, protecting against worst-case scenarios
